text: All subcontractors on our work sites and elsewhere in the performance of their work must comply with the safety and health regulations established by GS Mountain, OSHA, state OSHA programs, any applicable ANSI standards as well as any site-specific programs that may be contractually required. Subcontractors must have their own written safety program which should include, but not be limited to, hazard recognition, safety meetings, required Pre-Task Planning, proper use of tools and equipment, hazard communications, frequent inspections, accident investigation, and other aspects discussed in this document. Subcontractors with lower tiers of subcontractors have the total responsibility to see that their lower tiers also comply with the Contractor's requirements.
